---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->


# AdvRPkgDevel

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The goal of AdvRPkgDevel is to provide a mechanism for me to practise the techniques described in https://andyteucher.ca/dfo-advanced-r-pkgs-2026/package-fundamentals.html

## Installation

You can install the development version of AdvRPkgDevel from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("MMMcMahon/AdvRPkgDevel")
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to solve a common problem:

```{r example}
if (requireNamespace("AdvRPkgDevel", quietly = TRUE)) {
  library(AdvRPkgDevel)
} else {
  devtools::load_all(".")
}
cpue(catch = 100, effort = 10)

# With gear standardization
cpue(catch = 100, effort = 10, gear_factor = 0.5)
```

What is special about using `README.Rmd` instead of just `README.md`? You can include R chunks.

You'll still need to render `README.Rmd` regularly, to keep `README.md` up-to-date. `devtools::build_readme()` is handy for this.

You can also embed plots.

In that case, don't forget to commit and push the resulting figure files, so they display on GitHub and CRAN.
